summary: Crypto refactor testing complete. All 41 crypto unit tests pass. All 18 API integration tests pass. Backend starts cleanly with crypto service initialized (v2=False, kid=v1, bypass=False). Auth login works. Channel manager provider endpoints work. No credential leakage in API responses. All legacy module imports functional.
critical_code_review_comments: Crypto refactor is well-designed with single encryption boundary (CredentialEncryptionService)
All 6 legacy modules properly delegate to core.crypto.service
Phase 0 mode (v2=false) uses legacy aes256gcm: format for backward compatibility
AAD context binding provides tamper detection for tenant/provider/property isolation
Key rotation with dual-key support (CM_MASTER_KEY_CURRENT + CM_MASTER_KEY_PREVIOUS) works
Legacy format compatibility (XOR, old AES-GCM, base64) preserved for migration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Crypto refactor (SEC-002) is complete and tested. 41 unit tests in test_crypto_engine.py cover: encrypt/decrypt roundtrip, tamper detection, key rotation, legacy format compatibility, re-encryption, masking, no-secret-leak, envelope parsing, feature flags, dict operations, and key derivation. 18 API integration tests in test_crypto_refactor_api.py verify: crypto service initialization, legacy module imports, auth login, channel manager providers, HotelRunner/Exely connection endpoints, and no credential leakage. All tests pass. Phase 0 mode (CRYPTO_V2_ENABLED=false) is active.
